# CDSS Pediatric Clinical Decision Support System — Full Description

## Overview
An open-source pediatric clinical decision support system (CDSS) that runs entirely in the browser.
It integrates with electronic health record systems via the SMART on FHIR standard and requires
zero backend infrastructure. Deployed as a static site on GitHub Pages.

Target users: pediatric nurses, physicians, and caregivers managing children aged 0–6 years.
Target age groups: infant (0–12 months), toddler (1–3 years), preschool (3–6 years).

## URL
https://yao.care/smart-pedi-cds/

## Source
https://github.com/yaocare/smart-pedi-cds

---

## Functional Areas

### 1. SMART on FHIR Connectivity
- Supports both EHR Launch (embedded in EHR iframe) and Standalone Launch flows
- Uses fhirclient.js v2 for OAuth2 PKCE authentication and FHIR R4 resource access
- Reads Patient, Observation, and Encounter resources from the FHIR server
- Observation resources are filtered by LOINC code to extract pediatric vital signs

### 2. AI-Powered Risk Alerting
- Dual-layer alert engine: deterministic rule engine + ONNX Runtime Web ML model
- Rule engine reads threshold tables keyed by age group and indicator
- Risk levels: normal, advisory, warning, critical (4 levels)
- ML inference runs in a Web Worker to avoid blocking the main thread
- ONNX model file loaded at runtime; fallback to rule engine if model unavailable
- Multi-indicator logic: 2+ concurrent advisory readings escalate to warning automatically
- Trend detection: consecutive-day deterioration triggers escalation

### 3. Closed-Loop Decision Workflow
- Alert generated → clinician reviews → accepts/modifies/rejects recommendation
- All workflow state stored in IndexedDB (Dexie.js) for offline persistence
- Deduplication window (configurable, default 30 minutes) prevents alert storms
- Escalation timers: advisory → warning after N hours, warning → critical after M hours
- Missing-data alerts: fires if no new readings received within configured threshold

### 4. Clinical Dashboard
- D3.js trend charts for all 7 monitored indicators over selectable time windows
- Color-coded risk bands overlaid on charts (normal / advisory / warning / critical)
- Sparkline summary cards for quick at-a-glance status
- Responsive layout built with Svelte 5 island components inside Astro 5 SSG pages
- Print-ready PDF report generation via jsPDF

### 5. Patient Education Content System
- Markdown-based content collection managed via Astro Content Collections
- Content schema enforces: title, summary, category, ageGroup, format, publishedAt, locale
- Categories: diet, sleep, respiratory, exercise, milestone, general
- Formats: article, video, questionnaire
- Content is statically rendered at build time; no runtime CMS dependency
- RSS feed available at /smart-pedi-cds/rss.xml for feed readers and LLM crawlers
- Pagefind-indexed for full-text client-side search (zh-TW language)

### 6. PDF Report Generation
- One-click export of current patient dashboard state to PDF
- Includes: patient demographics, risk summary, indicator trend charts, active alerts
- Generated entirely client-side using jsPDF; no server upload required
- Output formatted for A4 printing with Chinese font support

---

## Data Model

### Monitored Indicators (7 total)
| Indicator          | LOINC Code  | Unit    |
|--------------------|-------------|---------|
| Heart rate         | 8867-4      | bpm     |
| SpO2               | 59408-5     | %       |
| Respiratory rate   | 9279-1      | /min    |
| Body temperature   | 8310-5      | °C      |
| Sleep quality      | (custom)    | score   |
| Activity level     | (custom)    | score   |
| Sugar intake       | (custom)    | g/day   |

### Age Groups (3 total)
- infant: 0–12 months
- toddler: 13–36 months
- preschool: 37–72 months

### Risk Levels (4 total)
- normal: within expected physiological range
- advisory: mild deviation; monitor closely
- warning: significant deviation; clinical review recommended
- critical: immediate intervention required

---

## Architecture

- Build tool: Astro 5 (SSG, static output)
- UI components: Svelte 5 (islands hydration)
- Charting: D3.js v7 (d3-selection, d3-scale, d3-shape, d3-axis, d3-time)
- Client storage: Dexie.js 4 (IndexedDB wrapper)
- ML runtime: ONNX Runtime Web 1.x (WebAssembly + WebGL backends)
- FHIR client: fhirclient 2.x
- Search: Pagefind 1.x (WASM, runs in browser, zero server)
- PDF: jsPDF 4.x
- Deployment: GitHub Actions → GitHub Pages (zero-cost hosting)
- CI: pnpm + astro check + eslint + lychee link checker

## Content Structure

src/data/education/        — Markdown education articles
src/data/rules/            — YAML threshold rule tables (per age group, per indicator)
src/data/baselines/        — JSON population baseline statistics (mean, std, percentiles)
src/pages/education/       — Astro pages rendered from education collection
src/pages/rss.xml.ts       — RSS feed endpoint for education content
public/llms.txt            — Brief LLM discovery file
public/llms-full.txt       — This file; comprehensive system description
pagefind.yml               — Pagefind search index configuration
